subject: Geography
title: Water Crises in Cities
description: A quick win: understand Water Crises in Cities.

hook.text: In 2018, Cape Town nearly became the first major city to run out of water completely.

buildup.text: 'Day Zero'—the day taps would be shut off—was weeks away before rains finally came.

discovery.text: Residents were limited to 50 liters per day. Showers lasted under two minutes.

twist.text: Chennai, São Paulo, and Mexico City have all faced similar crises in recent years.

climax.text: Megacities are draining aquifers, rivers, and reservoirs faster than nature can refill them.

punchline.text: The biggest threat to megacities isn't war. It's thirst.

quiz.question: What was 'Day Zero' in Cape Town?
options: The projected day the city's taps would be shut off
The day a new dam was opened
The anniversary of the city's founding
